title: Broadcom Symantec SEP Network Threat Protection IPS Event Correlation
dataSources: `sourcetype=symantec:ep:security:file` Network Threat Protection / IPS-style network attack events from SEP clients
app: Splunk Add-on for Symantec Endpoint Protection (Splunkbase 2772)
spl:
index=endpoint sourcetype="symantec:ep:security:file" earliest=-24h
| eval desc=coalesce(Event_Description, event_description, _raw)
| where match(lower(desc), "(?i)intrusion|ips|network threat|port scan|denial of service|exploit|network attack")
| eval host=coalesce(Computer_Name, computer_name, host)
| eval remote=coalesce(Remote_IP, remote_ip, Attacker_IP, attacker_ip, src_ip, "")
| stats count values(desc) as event_samples by host remote
| where count>=5
| sort -count
description: SEP’s network layer can block exploits and reconnaissance before payloads touch disk. Correlating repeated IPS-style events by internal host and external peer distinguishes broad scanning from targeted lateral movement precursors.
value: Improves network-aware detection at the endpoint tier and prioritizes hosts seeing sustained attack traffic for firewall or EDR follow-up.
implementation: (1) Confirm Network Threat Protection events reach Splunk with attacker and victim IP fields. (2) Enrich `remote` with threat intel ASN and reputation. (3) Threshold for burst detection (for example five or more events per pair per day). (4) Join to perimeter firewall logs for corroboration.
visualization: Table (host, remote IP, count), link analysis diagram, choropleth or geomap if geoip available.

references.title: Splunk Add-on for Symantec Endpoint Protection

grandmaExplanation: We group Broadcom Symantec’s network attack warnings to see which computers are under repeated probing or exploit attempts from the internet.
